messages.content:
The Eiffel Tower is an iconic iron lattice tower located in Paris, France. It was built for the 1889 World's Fair, held to commemorate the 100th anniversary of the French Revolution, and was intended to be a temporary structure. However, it became an instant symbol of Paris and France, and it has remained one of the most recognizable landmarks in the world.

The Eiffel Tower stands at a height of 324 meters (1,063 feet) and was the tallest man-made structure in the world when it was first built. It was designed and constructed by Gustave Eiffel and his engineering company, and it took approximately 2 years and 2 months to complete.

The tower is made up of over 18,000 pieces of wrought iron, weighing a total of around 7,300 tons. It is held together by over 2.5 million rivets and is designed to be flexible, allowing it to sway slightly in the wind.

Today, the Eiffel Tower is one of the most popular tourist attractions in the world, with over 7 million visitors per year. It offers stunning views of the city of Paris from its observation decks, which are located on the first and second floors, as well as a restaurant and a champagne bar on the top floor.

The Eiffel Tower has also become a cultural icon, symbolizing French culture and engineering ingenuity. It has been featured in countless films, literature, and artwork, and continues to inspire wonder and awe in people from around the world.
